sections.paragraph:
σωμάτιον, ου, τό (Isocr. et al.; pap) dim. of σῶμα.

① little body, esp. poor (little) body (Socrat., Ep. 30, 1; 31; Epict. 1, 1, 10; 24; 1, 9, 2 al., s. the index of Schenkl’s ed.; Lucian, Jupp. Trag. 41; Philo, Leg. ad Gai. 273) lit., of the tortured body of a martyr MPol 17:1.

② a transcendent body, body, fig. ext. of 1, the ‘body’ of a persecuted church ISm 11:2 v.l. (for σωματεῖον ‘corporate body’, the rdg. of Bihlmeyer’s text; s. L-S-J-M and Lightfoot’s note ad loc.).—DELG s.v. σῶμα. M-M. s.v. σῶμα.

Isocr Isocr , IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

dim. dim. = diminutive

esp. esp. = especially

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)
  2. ed. = edited (by), edition

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

ext. ext. = extension

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

rdg. rdg. = reading(s)

Bihlmeyer Bihlmeyer = KB., Die Apostolischen Väter—List 6

L-S-J-M L-S-J-M = (L-S-J) HLiddell/RScott, A Greek-English Lexicon; New Edition by HSJones/RMcKenzie—List 6

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 984
